definition: For node-level graph tasks, represent each sample by its k-hop neighborhood and compute Jaccard overlap between evaluation and training neighborhoods. This audits whether performance depends on local graph topology or train-neighbor proximity rather than transferable features.
example_usage: python graph_neighborhood_similarity.py --edges edges.csv --train train_nodes.csv --eval eval_nodes.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --k 2

citations.title: 2-hop Neighbor Class Similarity (2NCS): A graph structural metric indicative of graph neural network performance

evidence.summary: The paper introduces a 2-hop neighborhood metric that correlates with GNN performance.
The metric is motivated by one-hop and two-hop label propagation behavior.
GOOD defines graph OOD splits and shows performance gaps under covariate and concept shifts.

limitations: Neighborhood overlap is node-level and may not apply to whole-graph similarity without graph kernels or graph embeddings.
High overlap can reflect leakage in transductive graphs rather than meaningful scientific similarity.
The choice of k changes the locality and sparsity of the axis.
quality_gates: Report whether the graph is directed, whether edges are temporal, and which k is used.
Avoid using test labels or post-split edges unavailable at training time.
Compare topology similarity against feature or embedding similarity when node attributes exist.
